In light of this hadith, we once again realize how valuable and meaningful the surahs recited in prayer are.

Detailed Lists and Applications

Many individuals may experience a lack of information regarding the order and recitation of zamm-ı surahs. Here are detailed insights related to these surahs:

  • Surah Al-Fatiha: The surah that is obligatory to be recited in every rak'ah of prayer. Arabic: "Bismillahirrahmanirrahim. Elhamdulillahi rabbil alemin..." Translation: "In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. All praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the worlds..."
  • Surah Al-Ikhlas: Considered a summary of monotheism. Arabic: "Kul huvallahu ehad..." Translation: "Say: He is Allah, the One. Allah, the Eternal Refuge..."
  • Surah Al-Kafirun: Symbolizes fidelity in faith. Arabic: "Kul ya eyyuhel kafirun..." Translation: "Say: O disbelievers! I do not worship what you worship..."

Each of these surahs plays a significant role in enhancing the spiritual depth of prayer and strengthening our closeness to Allah.

Lessons from Scholars

Islamic scholars have expressed many wise sayings regarding the correct understanding and internalization of the surahs. Imam Ghazali stated, "Prayer is the place where the heart meets Allah", emphasizing that the recitation of the surahs should be done not only with the tongue but also with the heart. This understanding allows the zamm-ı surahs to have an impact on our souls.

Conclusion

In conclusion, the order and correct recitation of prayer surahs require spiritual discipline and awareness. Each surah adorns our prayers as a manifestation of our submission and devotion to Allah. With the information shared in this article, may you experience a deeper sense of humility and connection in your prayers. May Allah accept our prayers and always inspire us on the right path.

baslik: Sekine Prayer and Verses: A Remedy for Spiritual Distress
seo_meta_aciklama: The place of Sekine prayer and verses in overcoming spiritual distress according to the Qur'an and Sunnah, methods of application, and the insightful stories of scholars.
icerik_html:
Introduction

In the complexities of life, our souls often seek peace. When our hearts feel constricted, we embark on a quest for a spiritual refuge. In such moments, Sekine prayer emerges as a powerful means of overcoming spiritual distress. This prayer is a spiritual remedy that brings tranquility to hearts and serenity to minds.

Historical/Religious Background

The Sekine prayer is a practice with deep roots in the Qur'an and Sunnah. Sekine, in its literal sense, means 'peace' and 'serenity'. In the Qur'an, this concept is mentioned as a symbol of the spiritual tranquility that Allah grants to hearts.

"He is the One who sends down tranquility into the hearts of the believers..." (Surah Al-Fath, Verse 4)

This verse clearly expresses how Allah instills a sense of peace and security into the hearts of the believers. Our Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) frequently recited this prayer during times of distress and recommended it to his community.

Detailed Lists and Applications

For those wishing to recite the Sekine prayer, the Arabic text, its pronunciation, and its Turkish meaning are provided below:

  • Arabic: اللّهُمَّ أَنْزِلْ عَلَينَا سَكِينَتَك
  • Pronunciation: Allahumme enzil aleyna sekinetek
  • Turkish Meaning: O Allah, send down Your peace and tranquility upon us.

This prayer can be recited three times after the morning and evening prayers. Additionally, whenever you feel stressed or uneasy, you can sincerely recite this prayer to seek refuge in Allah.

Lessons from Scholars

The great Islamic scholar Imam Ghazali emphasized the spiritual effects of the Sekine prayer, highlighting that peace and serenity can only be attained by seeking refuge in Allah. One day, when one of his students experienced a spiritual crisis, he recommended this prayer and said:

"When your heart feels constricted, turn to Allah's tranquility and ask Him for Sekine. Hearts find solace only through the remembrance of Allah."

Conclusion

In the challenging moments of life, turning to Allah and reciting the Sekine prayer is one of the most effective ways to overcome spiritual imbalances. This prayer invites Allah's peace and security into our lives. Let us pray that our hearts be filled with tranquility and our minds find serenity.

baslik: The Secrets of the Night of Qadr, More Virtuous than a Thousand Months
seo_meta_aciklama: A comprehensive guide on the Night of Qadr and its spiritual depths. Discover this night that will add meaning to your life.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The Night of Qadr is one of the most profound spiritual nights in Islamic belief. This night is known as the night when the Qur'an was first revealed, and it is stated to be more virtuous than a thousand months in the sight of Allah. Understanding the deep significance of this special night, which brings peace and tranquility to the human heart, ignites a beacon of hope in every Muslim's heart. The Night of Qadr is not just a night; it is also a symbol of the boundless manifestation of divine mercy and forgiveness.

Historical/Religious Background

In the Qur'an, Surah Al-Qadr elaborates on the importance of this night. Allah has explicitly stated that He has made this night "more virtuous than a thousand months." This statement is a great blessing and an opportunity for believers. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) recommended seeking this night during the last ten days of Ramadan. According to narrations, on this night, angels and Gabriel (peace be upon him) descend to the earthly realm by the permission of Allah, spreading peace and tranquility.

"Indeed, We sent it (the Qur'an) down on the Night of Qadr. And what can make you know what is the Night of Qadr? The Night of Qadr is better than a thousand months." (Surah Al-Qadr, 97:1-3)

Detailed Lists and Practices

The acts of worship and prayers performed on the Night of Qadr deepen a person's spiritual journey. Here are some prayers that can be recited on the Night of Qadr:

  • In Arabic: اللّهُمَّ اِنَّكَ عَفُوٌّ تُحِبُّ الْعَفْوَ فَاعْفُ عَنِّي
    Transliteration: Allahumme inneke afuvvun tuhibbul afve fa'fu anni
    Translation: O Allah! You are the Most Forgiving, and You love forgiveness; so forgive me.
  • This prayer is among the most recommended by our Prophet (peace be upon him) on the Night of Qadr. Reciting this prayer abundantly to revive this night is one of the best ways to seek forgiveness from Allah.

Lessons from Scholars

Islamic scholars have expressed many valuable words regarding the spiritual significance of the Night of Qadr. Imam Ghazali stated that the heart of the one who revives this night will be filled with deep love and submission to Allah. Imam Rabbani emphasized that sincere prayers made on this night will be accepted by Allah. The Night of Qadr is an opportunity for renewal and purification for those who turn their hearts entirely towards Allah.

Conclusion

The Night of Qadr not only revives a single night but also provides a spiritual elevation that will shape the rest of our lives. Every Muslim should seek refuge in Allah's mercy on this special night, aiming to purify themselves from sins and make a new beginning. To best revive this night, we must keep our hearts and intentions pure and strengthen our sincerity towards Allah. With Allah's permission, we pray that the supplications made on this night are accepted and bring blessings into our lives...

baslik: The Virtue of Completing the Qur'an and the Importance of the Completion Prayer
seo_meta_aciklama: A comprehensive examination of the spiritual depths of completing the Qur'an and the significance of the completion prayer.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The Qur'an is the divine guidance and source of wisdom that lies at the center of the lives of Muslims. Completing it is a spiritual journey for every reader; it is one of the deepest ways to draw closer to Allah, purify the heart, and find spiritual tranquility. Reading the Qur'an from beginning to end allows each verse to resonate in the heart and leaves deep impressions of each word on the soul. This spiritual journey is an act of worship that elevates the reader both in this world and the Hereafter.

Historical/Religious Background

The Qur'an is the last divine book revealed to our Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). Completing it is considered one of the Sunnahs of our Prophet. He emphasized the importance of reading the Qur'an by saying, "Read the Qur'an, for it will come as an intercessor on the Day of Resurrection" (Muslim). Reading the Qur'an from beginning to end is not only an individual act of worship but also an action that strengthens the spiritual unity of the Ummah.

Detailed Lists and Practices

The Virtue of the Completion Prayer

The completion prayer is a special prayer performed after completing the Qur'an. This prayer is an opportunity to ask Allah for the wisdom and blessings of the Qur'an, to seek forgiveness for mistakes, and to spiritually renew oneself.

  • Arabic: "Allahümme erhamnî bil-Kur'ân ve'c'alhû lî imâmen ve nuren ve hûden ve rahmeh. Allahümme zekkirnî minhü mâ nesîtü ve 'allimnî minhü mâ cehiltü ve'rzuknî tilâvetehû ânâe'l-leyl ve ânâe'n-nehâr ve'c'alhû lî hücceten yâ Rabb'el-âlemîn!"
  • Pronunciation: "Allahümme erhamnî bil-Kur'ân ve’c’alhû lî imâmen ve nuren ve hûden ve rahmeh. Allahümme zekkirnî minhü mâ nesîtü ve ‘allimnî minhü mâ cehiltü ve’rzuknî tilâvetehû ânâe’l-leyl ve ânâe’n-nehâr ve’c’alhû lî hücceten yâ Rabb’el-âlemîn!"
  • Translation: "O Allah, have mercy on me through the Qur'an, and make it for me a guide, light, guidance, and mercy. Remind me of what I have forgotten from it, teach me what I do not know, and grant me the ability to recite it during the night and during the day, and make it a proof for me, O Lord of the worlds!"

Lessons from Scholars

The great Islamic scholar Imam Ghazali said, "Reading the Qur'an removes heedlessness from the heart, and completing it revives the heart," highlighting the spiritual effects of reading and completing the Qur'an. Similarly, Imam Malik stated that reading the Qur'an is one of the best ways to direct the heart towards Allah. The words of these great figures teach us the spiritual depths of engaging with the Qur'an.

Conclusion

Completing the Qur'an and the subsequent completion prayer open the doors to spiritual elevation and tranquility. This act of worship strengthens our connection to Allah and purifies our hearts. O reader, make this divine word a guide in your life, read it, reflect upon it, and live by it. Allah loves and rewards those who read and apply the Qur'an. To intend to embark on this journey is a means of attaining Allah's mercy. May my Lord make the Qur'an a guide for us in every moment of our lives. Ameen.

baslik: The Stages of the Self in Sufism: A Spiritual Journey from Emmare to Mutmainne
seo_meta_aciklama: Explore the layers of the self in Sufism; delve into the depths of the spiritual journey from Emmare to Mutmainne.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The journey of self-discovery is as profound and meaningful as the exploration of the external world. In Sufism, this journey is realized through the stages of the self. Each stage allows an individual to deepen within their inner world and strengthen their connection with the Creator. This spiritual journey encompasses a process from Emmare to Mutmainne; it includes the steps necessary for an individual to purify themselves and attain peace for their soul.

Historical/Religious Background

The term 'self' (nafs) is used in various meanings in the Qur'an, and different states of the self are discussed. The self is a profound concept related to the inner world of humans, and in Sufism, various degrees of this concept are defined.

"He who purifies his soul has succeeded." (Shams, 91:9)

Islamic scholars have presented the stages of the self as a guide to understand and overcome the inner states that a person will encounter in their spiritual journey. These stages express the processes of change and transformation that occur within an individual.

Detailed Lists and Practices

  • Nafs-i Emmare: The self that commands evil. At this stage, the individual succumbs to their inner desires and impulses.
    Verse: "Indeed, the self commands to evil." (Yusuf, 12:53)
  • Nafs-i Levvame: The self that blames itself. The individual becomes aware of their mistakes and feels remorse.
  • Nafs-i Mülhime: The self that receives inspiration. The individual is guided towards the right path by divine inspirations.
  • Nafs-i Mutmainne: The self that finds peace. The individual attains inner tranquility and fully surrenders to Allah.

For each stage of the self, prayers and remembrances have been recommended by Islamic scholars. Here are some of them:

  • "Ya Muqallibal-qulubi thabbit qalbi 'ala dinik": "O Allah, who turns the hearts, make my heart steadfast upon Your religion."
  • "Subhanallahi wa bihamdihi, subhanallahil-azim": "I glorify Allah with His praise, I glorify Allah, the Exalted."

Lessons from Scholars

Imam Ghazali is one of the Islamic scholars who has examined the stages of the self in detail. Ghazali viewed the process of training and purifying the self as part of a person's journey towards Allah.

"The heart can only turn towards Allah by distancing itself from the desires of the self." - Imam Ghazali

Mevlana Jalaluddin Rumi expressed that transcending the stages of the self is possible through love and surrender.

"He who serves his self is imprisoned by his ego; he who serves Allah is liberated." - Mevlana

Conclusion

The Sufi journey is a process of personal purification and closeness to Allah, traversing through the stages of the self. This spiritual journey from Emmare to Mutmainne enables an individual to attain inner peace. May Allah free us from the captivity of our selves and make us among those who attain tranquility. Ameen.

baslik: What is Tawakkul? The Spiritual Peace Derived from Entrusting All Matters to Allah
seo_meta_aciklama: The meaning of Tawakkul and its significance in Islam. The peace brought by trusting in Allah and entrusting all matters to Him.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The complexities and uncertainties of life can sometimes leave a person feeling weary and helpless. In these moments, one of the most effective ways to find inner peace and security is to practice tawakkul. Tawakkul means to trust in Allah in every matter, to expect the outcome from Him, and to submit. This concept, which opens the doors to spiritual tranquility, touches the deepest corners of the heart, granting a unique serenity to the individual.

Historical/Religious Background

The concept of tawakkul holds an important place in the Qur'an and the Sunnah of our Prophet (peace be upon him). Allah, the Exalted, praises His servants who place their trust in Him in the Qur'an and promises to assist them. In one verse, it is stated:

"Put your trust in Allah. Allah is sufficient as a Trustee." (Surah An-Nisa, Verse 81)

This verse demonstrates that tawakkul is not merely a piece of advice but also a divine command. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) has also narrated many hadiths regarding the importance of tawakkul. One of them states:

"If you truly placed your trust in Allah, you would be provided for like the birds that go out hungry in the morning and return full in the evening." (Tirmidhi)

Detailed Lists and Practices

To incorporate the concept of tawakkul into our lives, it is important to adopt certain prayers and practices. Here are some suggestions:

  • Tawakkul Prayer: "Hasbiyallahü lâ ilâhe illâ hû. Aleyhi tevekkeltü ve hüve rabbül arşil azîm."
    Meaning: "Allah is sufficient for me. There is no deity but Him. I have placed my trust in Him. He is the Lord of the Mighty Throne."
  • Practice: Begin and end your day by reciting this prayer every morning and evening. Direct your mind and heart towards Allah.
  • Daily Habit of Tawakkul: Make it a habit to trust in Allah and turn to Him in every step of your daily tasks.

Lessons from Scholars

Many great figures in Islamic history have deeply contemplated and lived the principle of tawakkul. Imam Ghazali explains the effect of tawakkul on the heart as follows:

"Tawakkul is the name of the heart's submission to Allah, not merely clinging to causes."

This understanding transforms tawakkul from being just a matter of faith into a comprehensive approach that encompasses all aspects of life.

Conclusion

Tawakkul provides unwavering confidence and peace in the face of life's challenges. This submission to Allah continually fills the heart with tranquility. By making it a habit to trust in Allah and seek refuge in Him in every moment and every task, we can attain spiritual peace. Our prayer is that Allah grants us the ability to be among His servants who always practice tawakkul.

baslik: Sincerity and Authenticity: The Essence of Deeds and Ways to Escape from Showiness (Riya)
seo_meta_aciklama: A comprehensive guide on the importance of sincerity and authenticity, the essence of deeds, and ways to escape from showiness.
icerik_html:
Introduction

One of the most precious values lying deep within the human soul is sincerity. Sincerity is the essence of every deed, worship, and human behavior. For a deed to be accepted by Allah, it must be performed with sincerity, that is, solely for the sake of seeking Allah's pleasure. In today's world, the number of deeds performed with authenticity has diminished, as the love for the world and showiness (riya) have ensnared people. However, it is possible to regain the sincerity that nourishes our souls and enlightens our hearts.

Historical/Religious Background

The importance of sincerity and authenticity is frequently emphasized in the Holy Quran. Allah the Exalted states, "Therefore, pray to your Lord and sacrifice (to Him alone)." (Surah Al-Kawthar, verse 2), indicating that deeds should be performed solely for Allah. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) also expressed the significance of sincerity in the acceptance of deeds in one of his hadiths: "Actions are judged by intentions. Each person will have what they intended." (Bukhari, Faith, 41).

Detailed Lists and Applications

  • Sincerity Prayer:
    اللّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ أَنْ أُشْرِكَ بِكَ شَيْئًا وَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ، وَأَسْتَغْفِرُكَ لِمَا لاَ أَعْلَمُ

    Pronunciation: Allahumme inni eûzu bike min en üşrike bike şey'en ve ene a'lemu ve estagfiruke lima la a'lemu.

    Translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from associating anything with You while I know, and I ask for Your forgiveness for what I do not know.

  • Authenticity Practices:
    • Constantly feeling ourselves in the presence of Allah and performing our deeds solely for His pleasure.
    • Correcting our intention before starting our acts of worship and ensuring that we act with sincerity.
    • Setting aside worldly interests and making the pleasure of Allah our primary goal in our deeds.

Lessons from Scholars

Great Islamic scholar Imam Ghazali defines sincerity as seeing with the eye of the heart and states, "Sincerity is the purification of the heart from everything other than Allah." Imam Rabbani emphasizes the importance of sincerity in spiritual life by saying, "Sincerity is the essence of deeds. Worship performed without sincerity resembles a soulless body."

Conclusion

In conclusion, sincerity and authenticity are the cornerstones of our spiritual life. By staying away from showiness and ostentation, we should direct our hearts solely towards seeking Allah's pleasure. We pray to our Lord to adorn us with sincerity and accept our deeds. "O Allah! Keep us away from all forms of showiness and grant us the ability to live solely for Your pleasure." Ameen.

baslik: The Station of Patience: The Great Reward of Not Rebelling Against Trials
seo_meta_aciklama: Patience is our greatest support in the face of trials; explore this virtuous teaching of Islam in depth.
icerik_html:
Introduction

Life sometimes confronts us with great tests at the most unexpected moments. These tests can manifest as material losses or as spiritual pains such as the loss of our loved ones. In these moments, the virtue of patience comes into play. Patience is not merely waiting, but the ability to stand firm in the face of events. In this article, we will deeply examine the spiritual significance of patience, its place in Islam, and the rewards of not rebelling against trials.

Historical/Religious Background

Patience is praised in many verses of the Qur'an and recommended to the believers. Allah the Exalted states,

"...Indeed, the reward for those who are patient will be given without account." (Az-Zumar, 10)
emphasizing the importance of patience. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) also highlighted in his hadiths the great virtue of showing patience. For instance, the hadith,
"Patience is half of faith." (Bukhari)
clearly shows the deep connection between patience and faith.

Patience in the Qur'an and Sunnah

  • Prophet Job (a.s.): He is one of the symbolic figures of patience. His steadfastness in the face of prolonged illness and losses teaches us how important patience is.
  • Prophet Jacob (a.s.): When he lost his son Joseph, he showed "a beautiful patience" and relied on Allah.

Detailed Lists and Applications

One of the finest examples of patience is seeking refuge in Allah through prayer. Here are some prayers that can be recited during times of trials:

  • Prayer 1:
    "Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return" (Al-Baqarah, 156)
    Arabic: إِنَّا لِلَّٰهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ
    Translation: "Indeed, we are Allah's and indeed to Him we shall return."
  • Prayer 2:
    "Allah is sufficient for us, and He is the best disposer of affairs"
    Arabic: حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
    Translation: "Allah is sufficient for us; He is the best protector."

Lessons from Scholars

Great Islamic scholars have shared many inspiring words and actions regarding patience. Imam Ghazali described patience as "the key to spirituality," while Rumi indicated the sweet outcome of patience by saying, "Patience is the honey of sorrows."

Conclusion

Not rebelling against trials is a manifestation of our faith in Allah. Patience strengthens our faith and matures us. We must wait patiently and seek refuge in Allah to be able to see the ease that comes with every hardship. May Allah grant us the ability to walk this path with patience and steadfastness. Ameen.

baslik: What is Seyrü Sülük? The Spiritual Journey of the Dervish Walking Towards the Truth
seo_meta_aciklama: Seyrü Sülük is the spiritual journey of the dervish walking towards the Truth. A profound exploration in the light of the Qur'an and Sunnah.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The journey into the spiritual depths of humanity has been a quest that has existed since the earliest times in history. This journey is referred to by the Sufis as Seyrü Sülük. This spiritual voyage, which is a means of drawing closer to the Truth, is an exploration into the inner worlds of the dervishes, a venture in pursuit of the truth residing in their hearts. This path, which embodies patience, surrender, and love at every step, is a courageous leap from the darkness of the soul towards the divine light.

Historical/Religious Background

Seyrü Sülük is one of the cornerstones of Islamic Sufism. In the Qur'an and in the Sunnah of our Prophet (peace be upon him), it is encouraged that humans are in a constant quest for spiritual perfection. Allah, the Exalted, states in the Qur'an:

"Indeed, for those who turn to Allah with a sincere heart, there is a great reward with Him. (Ash-Shura, 25)"

This verse emphasizes the significance of turning one's heart towards Allah. In many of the hadiths of our Prophet (peace be upon him), it has been emphasized that believers should purify their hearts and draw closer to Allah.

Detailed Practices and Applications

In the journey of Seyrü Sülük, there are specific practices and prayers that the dervishes follow.

  • Tawhid Dhikr: The recitation of "La Ilaha Illallah" is an important practice that connects the hearts of the dervishes to Allah.
  • Salawat al-Sharif: The salawat recited as "Allahümme salli alâ Muhammedin ve alâ âli Muhammed" is a way to remember our Prophet (peace be upon him) and to attain his intercession.
  • Istighfar: The istighfar beginning with "Estağfirullah el Azim" is one of the most sincere ways to seek purification from sins and to ask for forgiveness from Allah.

Lessons from Scholars

Many great figures in the history of Sufism have left significant marks on the journey of Seyrü Sülük. One of them is Mevlana Jalaluddin Rumi. Mevlana emphasizes the importance of the purity of the heart and the role of the tongue in this purity by saying, "The heart is the ocean, the tongue is the shore. Whatever is in the ocean washes up on the shore."

"Do not be deceived by appearances; look to the truth. Purify your soul so that you may be filled with Allah's light." - Mevlana Jalaluddin Rumi

Conclusion

Seyrü Sülük is the effort of the dervish to reach the Truth. Patience is hidden in every step, and love is concealed in every prayer. Those who embark on this journey aim to fill their hearts with the light of Allah by purifying themselves. We wish our readers to walk on this spiritual path with hearts filled with patience and surrender, striving to draw closer to Allah at every moment. May my Lord make us among those who draw near to Him.

baslik: The Etiquette of Dhikr: Islamic Methods for Purifying the Heart by Remembering Allah
seo_meta_aciklama: Purify your heart by remembering Allah. Discover the etiquette of dhikr in the light of the Qur'an and Sunnah.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The religion of Islam offers a profound teaching that nourishes the spiritual and moral aspects of a person. Within this teaching, dhikr is considered one of the most effective ways to direct and purify the heart towards Allah. Dhikr is not merely the act of remembering Allah with the tongue, but it also creates a state of tranquility and submission that penetrates the depths of the heart. The person who engages in this spiritual practice learns to feel the presence of Allah at all times, thus freeing themselves from worldly concerns.

Historical/Religious Background

The practice of dhikr holds a significant place in the Qur'an and Sunnah. In the Qur'an, it is stated that remembering Allah brings peace to the human heart:

'Do you not know that hearts find peace in the remembrance of Allah?' (Rad, 13/28)
Our Prophet (peace be upon him) also encouraged his community regarding dhikr, saying:
'The one who remembers Allah and the one who does not are like the living and the dead.' (Bukhari, Da'awat, 66)
These statements reveal how important and indispensable the practice of dhikr is as an act of worship.

Detailed Lists and Practices

Dhikr can be performed in various forms and methods. Here are the most common practices of dhikr:

  • Subhanallah: 'Allah is free from all imperfections.' This dhikr is recited to acknowledge Allah's glory and perfection. In Arabic: سبحان الله
  • Elhamdülillah: 'All praise is due to Allah.' It expresses feelings of gratitude and thankfulness. In Arabic: الحمد لله
  • Allahu Ekber: 'Allah is the Greatest.' It constantly reminds us of Allah's power and greatness. In Arabic: الله أكبر
  • La ilahe illallah: 'There is no deity but Allah.' It forms the essence of the belief in Tawhid. In Arabic: لا إله إلا الله

Lessons from Scholars

The great Islamic scholar Imam Ghazali describes the effects of the practice of dhikr on the soul as follows:

'Dhikr polishes the heart, allowing the light of Allah to shine within it. The servant, by constantly remembering Allah, is reminded that they are in the presence of Allah and lives with this awareness.'
These statements emphasize that dhikr is not merely a phrase, but a spiritual action that penetrates the depths of the heart.

Conclusion

Purifying our hearts by remembering Allah is one of the most effective ways to find tranquility amidst the chaos of worldly life. Dhikr is not just an act of worship; it is also a way of life. Let us remember Allah in every moment of our lives and instill this spiritual peace in our hearts. O my Lord, grant us the ability to find peace through Your remembrance and fill our hearts with You.

baslik: Asceticism and Piety: The Islamic Measure of Not Being Attached to the World with the Heart
seo_meta_aciklama: Discover the profound meanings of Asceticism and Piety in Islam; learn the ways to free oneself from the love of the world and the secrets to finding spiritual tranquility.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The worldly life presents itself as a captivating and often deceptive center of attraction. Asceticism and piety are the Islamic paths to escaping this allure and attaining true tranquility. Asceticism is the indifference of the heart towards worldly pleasures, while piety is the awareness of responsibility towards Allah. Both guide a person to establish a balance between this world and the Hereafter.

Historical/Religious Background

Asceticism and piety are concepts frequently emphasized in the Qur'an and Sunnah. In the Qur'an, Allah states, "The worldly life is but a game and amusement." (Al-An'am, 6:32) highlighting the transience of worldly life. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) also summarizes our attitude towards worldly life in a hadith, saying, "Be like a stranger or a traveler in this world." (Bukhari)

Detailed Lists and Applications

  • Supplications:
    • Allahümme inni es'elükel-hüdâ vet-tukâ vel-afâfe vel-ginâ.
      (O Allah, I ask You for guidance, piety, chastity, and richness of the heart.)
    • Okunuş: "Allahümme inni es'elükel-hüdâ vet-tukâ vel-afâfe vel-ginâ."
    • Meal: "O Allah, I ask You for guidance, piety, chastity, and richness of the heart." (Muslim)

Lessons from Scholars

Imam Ghazali stated about asceticism, "Asceticism is the emptying of the heart from the world and the settling of the love for the Hereafter in its place." The great scholar Hasan al-Basri emphasized the depth of this concept by saying, "Piety is the fear that the heart feels towards Allah."

Conclusion

In conclusion, asceticism and piety purify the Muslim's heart from the love of the world and strengthen their connection to Allah. These two concepts lead a person to true tranquility and spiritual perfection. O Lord, cleanse our hearts from the love of the world and make us among those who turn solely to You. Ameen.

baslik: The Spiritual Destruction of Arrogance and Pride: Escaping the Greatest Trap of Satan
seo_meta_aciklama: Discover the spiritual destruction of arrogance and pride, and learn ways to escape from Satan's greatest trap.
icerik_html:
Introduction

In every soul's journey, one of the greatest tests it faces is arrogance and pride. These two concepts are dangerous emotions that darken the heart, wear down the soul, and undermine one's closeness to Allah. Arrogance and pride are among the greatest obstacles one may encounter on the spiritual journey, and they are among Satan's favorite weapons. In this article, we will deeply examine the spiritual destruction of arrogance and pride and how we can escape from this trap.

Historical/Religious Background

The place of arrogance and pride in the Qur'an and Sunnah is quite clear. Since the creation of man, arrogance has always been seen as a destructive force. In the Qur'an, it is narrated that Satan was expelled from Allah's presence due to his arrogance:

"Allah said: 'What prevented you from prostrating when I commanded you?' Satan said: 'I am better than him; You created me from fire, and You created him from clay.'" (Al-A'raf, 7:12)

This verse is one of the clearest examples of how arrogance can lead a person to disaster. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) also stated the dangers of arrogance and pride:

"He who has an atom's weight of arrogance in his heart will not enter Paradise." (Muslim, Iman, 147)

Detailed Lists and Practices

Prayers and practices to rid oneself of arrogance and pride are very important. Here are some practices that can be done in this regard:

  • Repentance and Seeking Forgiveness: Seeking Allah's forgiveness is the first step to purifying oneself from arrogance. You can recite this prayer every day: "Astaghfirullah al-Azim" (Meaning: I seek forgiveness from the Most High Allah.)
  • Worship with Humility: Remembering our helplessness before Allah while prostrating in prayer is an effective method to break pride.
  • Charity and Generosity: Helping others disciplines our ego and weakens the feeling of arrogance.

Lessons from Scholars

Great Islamic scholars have pointed out the dangers of arrogance and pride. Imam Ghazali explains how arrogance takes root in a person's heart and how this emotion can be eradicated. He states:

"Arrogance is a disease of the heart. It can only be treated with humility."

These words clearly illustrate how dangerous arrogance is and the ways to overcome it.

Conclusion

Overcoming arrogance and pride is a spiritual journey. Each of us must strive to purify our hearts from such emotions. Let us not forget that the most valued in the sight of Allah is the one who finds humility deep within their heart. Pray, "O Allah, purify our hearts from arrogance and pride. Make us among those who are close to You. Ameen."

baslik: Self-Examination and Contemplation: Deepening by Reflecting on the Purpose of Creation
seo_meta_aciklama: Understanding the purpose of creation through self-examination and contemplation: A comprehensive guide on the profound spiritual practices of Islam.
icerik_html:
Introduction

Human beings require deep thought to understand the purpose of creation and to live accordingly. Self-examination and contemplation are spiritual practices that guide us in this thought process. In the silence of the heart, these two concepts invite us on an inner journey to find answers to the soul's quest. As we delve into our inner world, we find the opportunity to discover the meaning of life and what our Creator expects from us.

Historical/Religious Background

Self-examination and contemplation are among the fundamental spiritual disciplines of Islam. Many verses in the Qur'an invite people to think and take lessons. Allah states,

"They remember Allah while standing or sitting or [lying] on their sides and reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth." (Ali Imran, 191)
emphasizing the importance of contemplation. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) also stated,
"An hour of contemplation is better than a thousand years of voluntary worship."
indicating that thought is an action equivalent to worship.

Detailed Lists and Applications

How can we incorporate self-examination and contemplation into our lives? Here are some practical steps:

  • Setting Aside Time for Self-Examination: Dedicate a specific time of the day to think about Allah in a quiet and comfortable environment.
  • Contemplation Prayers: Regularly read verses from the Qur'an that encourage reflection. For example, frequently repeat the prayer "Hasbunallahu ve ni'mel vekil."
  • Self-Accountability: Reflect on your daily actions and their spiritual dimensions. Remember that every action done with good intention holds value in the sight of Allah.

Lessons from Scholars

Many great scholars have shared their profound thoughts on self-examination and contemplation. Imam Ghazali states that contemplation cleanses the heart and brings a person closer to the truth.

"Contemplation is the adornment of the heart and the light of the soul. Without it, a person remains in darkness,"
he says. These words highlight the role of thought in spiritual enlightenment.

Conclusion

Self-examination and contemplation are significant practices that bring a person closer to Allah and purify the soul. By incorporating these practices into our lives, we can better understand our purpose of creation and live accordingly. Let us not forget that every thought is a step towards Allah. We beseech our Lord to assist us on this path.

"Our Lord! Show us the truth and keep us steadfast on the right path."

baslik: Knowledge of Allah and Love of Allah: Knowing and Loving Allah with Affection
seo_meta_aciklama: A profound examination of knowing and loving Allah: The place of Marifetullah and Muhabbetullah in the Qur'an and Sunnah.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The purpose of human existence and the journey in this world is often shaped by returning to one's essence and recognizing the Creator. Marifetullah means knowing Allah; Muhabbetullah means loving Him with affection. These two concepts are the essence of spiritual life and touch the depths of the human heart. This journey is an invitation for a person to know themselves and draw closer to their Lord.

Historical/Religious Background

In the Qur'an and the Sunnah of our Prophet (peace be upon him), there are many evidences and encouragements regarding knowing and loving Allah. Allah the Exalted states, "I created the jinn and mankind only that they may worship Me." (Zariyat, 51/56), expressing that His servants were created to know and worship Him. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Love Allah, for He is your Creator and the One who bestows blessings upon you."

"Whoever memorizes the names of Allah will enter Paradise." (Bukhari)

Detailed Lists and Applications

For those who wish to progress on the path of Marifetullah and Muhabbetullah, certain prayers and remembrances are quite beneficial:

  • Prayer:
    Arabic: اللّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ حُبَّكَ وحُبَّ مَنْ يُحِبُّكَ وحُبَّ عَمَلٍ يُقَرِّبُنِي إِلَى حُبِّكَ
    Pronunciation: Allahümme inni es'elüke hubbeke ve hubbe men yuhibbuke ve hubbe amelin yukarribuni ila hubbike.
    Translation: O Allah, I ask You for Your love, the love of those who love You, and the love of actions that bring me closer to Your love.
  • Remembrance: The name "Ya Vedud" is a frequently recited remembrance to increase love and affection.

Lessons from Scholars

Imam Ghazali describes Marifetullah as follows: "True knowledge is the illumination of Allah's light in the heart of a person." When this light settles in the servant's heart, love for the world diminishes, and love and affection for Allah increase. Likewise, the great Sufi Rabia al-Adawiyya said, "Love for Allah leaves no room for any other love in the heart." These expressions illustrate how the love of Allah encompasses the entire being of a person and how love brings about a state of surrender.

Conclusion

Marifetullah and Muhabbetullah represent a process of transformation and rebirth in a person's life. This process purifies your heart, cleanses your soul, and brings you closer to Allah. We pray that Allah makes us among those who possess knowledge and love, and fills our hearts with His love. Ameen.

baslik: What is Ucub? The Dangers of a Person Being Pleased with Their Own Worship
seo_meta_aciklama: A comprehensive examination of the dangers of being pleased with one's own worship; from the perspectives of the Qur'an, Sunnah, and scholars.
icerik_html:
Introduction

A person turns to worship to deepen spiritually and attain inner peace. However, in this journey, they may unknowingly face a great danger: ucub. This is a heart disease that arises from a person being pleased with and boasting about their own worship. Ucub harms a person's spirituality and can distance them from Allah instead of bringing them closer. In this article, we will deeply examine how ucub creates a wound in our spiritual world and the ways to protect ourselves from it.

Historical/Religious Background

The dangers of ucub are clearly stated in both the Qur'an and the hadiths of our Prophet (peace be upon him). In the Qur'an, being pleased with oneself and acting with arrogance is condemned in many verses, and such behaviors are counted among the traits that Allah does not approve of. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) states:

"Whoever relies on their deeds, Allah leaves them to their deeds alone."

This hadith clearly illustrates how ucub can lead a person to solitude and deprive them of Allah's help.

Detailed Lists and Practices

There are certain prayers and practices that should be performed to protect oneself from ucub. Here are some of these prayers:

  • Arabic of the Prayers: "Allahümme ecirna minel ucub ve kibir."
  • Pronunciation: "O Allah, protect us from ucub and arrogance."
  • Translation: O Allah, protect us from self-admiration and arrogance.
  • How to Recite: This prayer should be recited at least three times a day to instill humility in the person's heart.

Lessons from Scholars

Islamic scholars have left us many sayings and stories that guide us regarding ucub. Imam Ghazali explains the effect of ucub on the heart as follows:

"Ucub is a disease that causes the heart to darken and worships to be rejected. Humility is the most effective remedy for this disease."

These words remind us of how dangerous ucub truly is and that the path to overcoming it lies in humility.

Conclusion

Recognizing ucub and protecting oneself from it is essential for our spiritual life. We should act with the intention of performing every act of worship solely for His pleasure, praying to the Almighty Allah to protect us from this disease. To conclude with a prayer:

"Our Lord, keep our hearts distant from ucub and arrogance, and reward us with the tranquility of being solely Your servants."

By carrying this prayer always on our tongues and in our hearts, we can continue our spiritual journey with more solid steps.

baslik: The Station of Contentment: Surrendering Heartily to Everything That Comes from Allah
seo_meta_aciklama: The station of contentment means to wholeheartedly accept everything that comes from Allah, reaching the pinnacle of patience and surrender.
icerik_html:
Introduction

Throughout human life, one encounters many difficulties and trials. The attitude shown in the face of these challenges determines a person's spiritual level. The station of contentment means to perceive and accept these challenges as Allah's decree and to surrender wholeheartedly. This profound spiritual concept is one of the cornerstones of leading a peaceful life.

Historical/Religious Background

Contentment holds an important place in the Qur'an and Sunnah. The verse in the Qur'an, "Allah is pleased with the believers, and they are pleased with Him" (Al-Bayyina, 8), emphasizes that contentment is a state of mutual acceptance and satisfaction. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) also indicated the importance of the station of contentment by saying, "Whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, they are with those whom Allah has blessed, the prophets, the truthful, the martyrs, and the righteous. What excellent companions they are!" (An-Nisa, 69).

Detailed Lists and Applications

  • Prayer of Contentment: "Allahümme inni es'elüke rizaka ve'l-cennete ve euzu bike min sahhatike ve'n-nar."
    Meaning: "O Allah, I seek Your contentment and Paradise, and I seek refuge in You from Your wrath and Hellfire." This prayer can be recited three times after the morning and evening prayers.
  • Prayer of Surrender: "Rabbena atina fiddünya haseneten ve fil ahireti haseneten ve kına azabennar."
    Meaning: "Our Lord! Grant us goodness in this world and goodness in the Hereafter, and protect us from the torment of the Fire." You can express your surrender with this prayer at the end of your daily worship.

Lessons from Scholars

Imam Ghazali emphasized the difficulty of reaching the station of contentment and stated: "Contentment is the purification of the heart from objection to Allah's decree." This saying shows that contentment is not merely acceptance but also a profound state of surrender. Furthermore, the words of Hz. Mevlana, "Everything will come to pass as it should," succinctly summarize the reflection of the station of contentment in daily life.

Conclusion

Reaching the station of contentment is the key to finding peace and happiness in one's life. Wholeheartedly accepting everything that comes from Allah provides an inner state of peace and surrender. I wish for everyone reading this article to seek Allah's contentment in every moment of their lives and to connect to Him with complete surrender. Remember, the station of contentment is the door of the heart that opens to Allah.

baslik: The Place of the Perfect Guide in Sufism and His Guiding Activities
seo_meta_aciklama: A profound examination of the spiritual guidance role of the Perfect Guide in Sufism and the significance of his guiding activities.
icerik_html:
Introduction

For those who embark on the journey to delve into the depths of the human soul, open the doors of the heart, and discover the truth, the Perfect Guide serves as a guiding light akin to a lighthouse. In Sufism, the guide is not merely a teacher who imparts knowledge but also a guiding mentor, a mirror for the heart, and a spiritual companion. In this article, we will deeply explore the place of the Perfect Guide and his guiding activities, enriching the topic with true stories that touch the souls.

Historical/Religious Background

The origins of Sufism are rooted in the profound understanding of the Qur'an and Sunnah. The concept of Friends of Allah, defined in the Qur'an as Allah's allies, is one of the cornerstones of Sufism. The relationship of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) with his companions also outlines the main principles of Sufism by modeling the guide-disciple relationship.

"Allah is the protector of the believers. He brings them out of darkness into light." (Al-Baqarah, 2:257)

Characteristics of the Perfect Guide

  • Intellectual Depth: The Perfect Guide is a person well-versed in the knowledge of the Qur'an and Sunnah, possessing intellectual depth.
  • Spiritual Maturity: He has disciplined his ego, has an open heart's eye, and has attained spiritual maturity.
  • Exemplary Morality: He embodies the morality of Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and serves as a role model in all aspects.

Detailed Lists and Practices

As they progress on the Sufi path, disciples often refer to certain prayers and remembrances. Here are some of them:

  • "Allahümme inni es’elüke hubbeke ve hubbe men yuhibbuk."

    Meaning: "O Allah, I ask You to love me and to love those who love You."

  • "La ilahe illallah, Muhammedün Resulullah."

    Meaning: "There is no deity but Allah, and Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah."

Lessons from Scholars

In the history of Sufism, the effects of the Perfect Guides on their disciples have created turning points in their lives. For example, Mevlana Jalaluddin Rumi experienced a spiritual revolution after meeting Shams of Tabriz and profoundly expressed this influence in his works.

"Yesterday I was clever, so I wanted to change the world. Today I am wise, so I am changing myself." - Mevlana

Conclusion

In conclusion, the guidance of the Perfect Guide plays a significant role in the enlightenment of the human soul. These individuals, who are the friends of Allah, illuminate our hearts and bring us out of darkness into light. We pray that everyone encounters a true guide on this path and can open the doors to the truth.

O Allah, who turns the hearts! Make our hearts steadfast in religion.

baslik: Methods Recommended by Scholars for the Purification of the Heart (Tasfiye-i Kalp)
seo_meta_aciklama: A comprehensive examination of the importance of heart purification and the methods recommended by scholars in this regard.
icerik_html:
Methods Recommended by Scholars for the Purification of the Heart (Tasfiye-i Kalp)

Introduction

The purification of the heart is a profound transformation in a person's spiritual life, a striving to reach the purest state of the soul. This endeavor allows one to become closer to Allah and find inner peace. In Islam, the heart is not only considered the center of emotions but also the source of faith. Therefore, the purification of the heart should be viewed as a spiritual obligation, an act of worship, and a way of life.

Historical/Religious Background

In Islam, the purification of the heart is a topic frequently emphasized in the Qur'an and Sunnah. Allah states in the Qur'an, "On that Day, neither wealth nor children will be of any benefit, except for those who come to Allah with a sound heart." (Surah Ash-Shu'ara, 88-89). The Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) also highlighted the importance of the heart by saying, "Indeed, there is a piece of flesh in the body; if it is sound, the whole body is sound; and if it is corrupt, the whole body is corrupt. That piece of flesh is the heart." (Bukhari, Iman, 39).

Detailed Lists and Practices

Among the methods recommended by scholars for the purification of the heart, supplications and dhikr hold significant importance.

  • Istiaze (Seeking Refuge Prayer): "Euzu billahi mineş-şeytanir-racim". Meaning: "I seek refuge with Allah from the accursed devil." This prayer should be recited frequently to cleanse the heart from ill intentions and thoughts.
  • İstiğfar (Seeking Forgiveness): "Estağfirullah el-azim". Meaning: "I seek forgiveness from Allah the Almighty." Regularly seeking forgiveness every day cleanses the sins that tarnish the heart.
  • Dhikr: "La ilahe illallah". Meaning: "There is no deity but Allah." This dhikr helps to purify the heart from worldly distractions and directs it towards Allah.

Lessons from Scholars

Imam Ghazali states about the purification of the heart: "The way to purify the heart is to rid it of bad traits. This can only be achieved through knowledge and action." Rumi emphasizes the importance of heart purification by saying, "Above all, keep the door of your heart clean so that Allah may be a guest there."

Conclusion

The purification of the heart is a spiritual journey that requires patience and determination. With Allah's permission, as we progress on this path, we can attain peace with a pure heart, becoming more purified each day. Let us not forget that the purification of the heart is not only an individual act of worship but also a social responsibility. I pray that our Lord grants us the ability to purify our hearts and cleanse our souls. Ameen.

baslik: What is Rabita? The Heart Connection and Spiritual Focus in Sufism
seo_meta_aciklama: Rabita is a method of focusing the heart on the guide and establishing a spiritual connection in Sufism. It is an effective way to achieve tranquility of the heart and draw closer to Allah.
icerik_html:
Introduction

Sufism is an inward journey towards the heart. In this journey, the spiritual connection that a person establishes with themselves and the Creator holds great significance. Rabita is considered one of the deepest paths of this connection. The human heart is viewed as a place where Allah's manifestations occur, and therefore, directing the heart towards Allah and contemplating it is an obligation for those who are knowledgeable in Sufism. Rabita emerges as one of the most effective methods of this spiritual focus and heart connection.

Historical/Religious Background

Rabita is a topic that is indirectly referenced in the Qur'an and deeply explored in the Sufi tradition. In the Qur'an, there are many verses indicating that closeness to Allah can be achieved through the heart. For example, "

They remember Allah while standing, sitting, and lying on their sides, and they reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth.
" (Ali Imran, 191). Sufi scholars have associated this verse with the spiritual meaning of rabita. In the Sunnah, the close relationship of our Prophet (peace be upon him) with his companions and his guidance towards their hearts are seen as examples of the spiritual connection that forms the basis of rabita.

Detailed Lists and Applications

  • Rabita Prayers:
    • Arabic: "Allahümme inni es'elüke bi nur-i vechikel kerim, en tec'ale fi kalbi nura ve fi basari nura ve fi sem'i nura."
    • Pronunciation: "Allahümme inni es'elüke bi nur-i vechikel kerim, en tec'ale fi kalbi nura ve fi basari nura ve fi sem'i nura."
    • Translation: "O Allah, by the honor of Your glorious face, grant light to my heart, light to my eyes, and light to my ears."
  • How to Perform Rabita:
    • Step 1: Sit in a quiet environment and close your eyes.
    • Step 2: Take a deep breath to calm your mind and heart.
    • Step 3: Try to connect your heart by thinking of your guide or Allah.
    • Step 4: Feel the tranquility of this spiritual connection and imagine your heart filled with Allah's light.

Lessons from Scholars

Great Sufis like Imam Rabbani have emphasized the importance of rabita for spiritual elevation, stating that "

Rabita serves as a means for the disciple to connect their heart to the heart of their guide, facilitating the path to Allah.
" Additionally, Abdul Qadir Gilani has stressed that rabita is necessary for the purification and cleansing of the heart.

Conclusion

Rabita is a guide in the journey of the heart towards Allah. This spiritual practice helps an individual find inner peace and enhances their closeness to Allah. O Allah, who turns the hearts, make our hearts steadfast upon the religion as we complete this spiritual journey. Let us not forget that the place our heart is directed towards is where our soul finds peace.

baslik: The Station of Fenafillah: Passing Beyond One's Existence and Vanishing in Allah
seo_meta_aciklama: The station of Fenafillah describes the process of annihilating oneself in Allah within the depths of Sufism.
icerik_html:
Introduction

One of the deepest quests of the human soul is to decipher the meaning of existence. Fenafillah is regarded as one of the highest stations in Sufism, signifying the annihilation of one's own existence in the existence of Allah. This spiritual journey involves purifying oneself from the ego and being filled with divine love. The station of Fenafillah represents a life lived solely for the pleasure of Allah, with all worldly desires erased from the heart.

Historical/Religious Background

The concept of Fenafillah is indirectly mentioned in the Qur'an and the Sunnah of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). Indeed, it is stated in the Qur'an:

"Say: My prayer, my acts of worship, my life, and my death are all for the Lord of the worlds, Allah." (Al-An'am 6:162)

This verse emphasizes that a believer must dedicate their entire existence to Allah. The Prophet (peace be upon him) also said:

"Be mindful of Allah, and you will find Him before you; remember Allah, and He will remember you."

This hadith reveals the close relationship between Allah and His servant and indicates that reaching the station of Fenafillah is indeed possible.

Detailed Lists and Practices

There are prayers and remembrances (dhikr) that can be performed to attain the station of Fenafillah. Here are some of them:

  • "La ilahe illallah" - This remembrance helps to purify the heart from everything except Allah.
  • "Subhanallah ve bihamdihi" - A prayer that glorifies Allah and acknowledges one's own insignificance in the face of His greatness.
  • "Allahümme ente maksudi ve rızake matlubi" - Meaning: "O Allah! My goal is You, and Your pleasure is my desire." This prayer reinforces the intention to live for Allah's pleasure.

Prayers and remembrances should be recited in a calm environment, with humility, after the morning and evening prayers.

Lessons from Scholars

The great Sufi master Mevlana Jalaluddin Rumi explains the station of Fenafillah with the following words:

"Love is such an expansive ocean that it has neither shore nor end. Those who dive into that ocean lose themselves and vanish in love."

Moreover, Hallaj al-Mansur's statement "Enel Hak" expresses his state of annihilation in Allah. This phrase has gone down in history as one of the most extreme examples of reaching the station of Fenafillah.

Conclusion

The station of Fenafillah is a peak that every believer may aspire to reach. It requires effort to earn Allah's pleasure and to unite with Him. We pray that our Lord enables us to purify our hearts from all worldly concerns and to focus solely on His pleasure. Every step we take on this path may bring us closer to Him. Ameen.

baslik: Halvet and Uzlet: Listening to the Self by Remaining Alone in Crowds
seo_meta_aciklama: Discover the spiritual depth of remaining alone in crowds by listening to the self through halvet and uzlet.
icerik_html:
Introduction

In the rapidly flowing tempo of the modern world, delving into the depths of the soul and listening to the self has become a need that we often overlook. Being able to remain alone in crowds signifies embarking on a spiritual journey. Halvet and uzlet constitute two important concepts of this journey in Islam. In this article, we aim to open a spiritual door for the reader by exploring the depths of these concepts.

Historical/Religious Background

In Islamic history, halvet and uzlet have been regarded as one of the ways to draw closer to Allah. There are verses in the Qur'an that embody the essence of these concepts. For example:

"Those who remember Allah while standing or sitting or [lying] on their sides and give thought to the creation of the heavens and the earth." (Âl-i İmrân, 3/191)

This verse emphasizes that a person should turn inward and reflect on the greatness of Allah. Our Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) often withdrew to the Cave of Hira to experience halvet and received revelation during this time.

Detailed Lists and Applications

Practices of halvet and uzlet become more meaningful with specific prayers and remembrances. Here are a few examples:

  • La ilahe illallah: This remembrance reminds us that there is no deity but Allah. It is recommended to be repeated at least 100 times a day.
  • Subhanallah, Elhamdulillah, Allahu Ekber: Each is recited 33 times, completing 100 at the end of the remembrances. It is a powerful remembrance for the training of the self.

Examples of Prayer

  • Arabic: اللهم اجعلني من عبادك المخلصين
    Pronunciation: Allahummec'alni min 'ibadikal muhlisin
    Translation: O Allah, make me among Your sincere servants.

Lessons from Scholars

Many scholars in Islamic history have emphasized the importance of halvet and uzlet. For instance, Imam Ghazali stated, "Those who cannot remain alone in crowds can never hear their inner voice," highlighting the significance of this concept. Similarly, Mevlana Jalaluddin Rumi expressed, "The voice of the crowds prevents us from hearing the voice of Allah," illustrating the necessity of an inward journey.

Conclusion

Halvet and uzlet are significant spiritual practices that nourish the soul, discipline the self, and facilitate closeness to Allah. At the end of this article, we would like to conclude with a prayer for the reader to embark on their own inner journey and experience remaining alone in crowds:

O Allah, grant us the ability to remain alone in crowds and to listen to our selves. Make us among those worthy of Your presence. Ameen.

baslik: Tears and Remorse: The Virtue of Repentance Washed with Tears
seo_meta_aciklama: The profound spiritual power of repentance and its effect on spiritual purification through tears.
icerik_html:
Introduction

Every human being experiences regrets and mistakes in their life. However, the repentance that follows these mistakes is a call resonating deep within the soul. Repentance is not merely a verbal expression, but a heartfelt return and purification. Repentance washed with tears cleanses the soul and brings the individual closer to Allah. In this article, we will deeply explore the spiritual significance of repentance and the role of tears in this process.

Historical/Religious Background

The place of repentance in the Qur'an and Sunnah is significant. Allah commands in the Qur'an, "O believers! Turn to Allah in sincere repentance" (Tahrim, 66/8), emphasizing that repentance should be performed with sincerity and earnestness. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) also stated, "The one who repents is like one who has never sinned" (Ibn Majah, Zuhd, 30), highlighting the forgiving power of repentance.

Detailed Lists and Practices

The supplications that can be recited while repenting hold an important place in a person's spiritual journey. Here are some of these supplications:

  • Arabic: "Rabbiğfir li ve tub 'aleyye inneke ente't-tevvâbu'r-rahîm."
  • Pronunciation: "Rabbiğfir li ve tub aleyye inneke ente't-tevvâbu'r-rahîm."
  • Translation: "My Lord! Forgive me, accept my repentance. Surely, You are the Most Accepting of Repentance and the Most Merciful."

This supplication can be recited sincerely after each prayer or at any time of the day.

Lessons from Scholars

Many great figures have experienced the importance of repentance and tears in their lives and have spoken wise words on this matter. Imam Ghazali stated, "Repentance is the revival of the heart. The heart returns to Allah through repentance and finds peace," drawing attention to the spiritual dimension of repentance.

An Exemplary Incident

One day, a man comes to Hasan al-Basri and asks, "O Hasan, I am a sinful servant, will Allah forgive me?" Hasan al-Basri replies, "The mercy of Allah is greater than the depths of the oceans. Repent and wash yourself with your tears; Allah will forgive you." These words leave a profound impact on the man's heart, and he sheds tears of remorse.

Conclusion

Every person has moments in their life when they need to repent. Seizing these moments and purifying oneself with tears provides spiritual renewal. Our Lord keeps the door of repentance open at all times; we just need to turn towards that door and make a heartfelt return. We can begin this journey by praying, "O Allah, make us among those who repent and cleanse our hearts with tears."

baslik: Self-Accountability: The Importance of Reckoning with Oneself Before Sleep
seo_meta_aciklama: A comprehensive guide on the spiritual significance of self-accountability and the ways to reckon with oneself before sleep.
icerik_html:
Introduction

Opening the doors to an inner journey, embarking on a quest deep within the soul... Self-accountability is precisely the concept that stands at the threshold of this door. At the end of each day, when the night envelops in silence, a person being alone with themselves and engaging in an internal reckoning has a purifying effect on the soul. This process is a fundamental building block of a person's spiritual elevation.

Historical/Religious Background

The religion of Islam encourages individuals to question themselves and engage in internal reckoning. The Quran frequently emphasizes the necessity for a person to train their soul. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) also provided various recommendations to his community regarding this matter:

"Reckon with yourselves before you are reckoned with."

This hadith clearly illustrates the significance of self-accountability in Islam. The companions of the Prophet and Islamic scholars have acted in light of this hadith, subjecting themselves to a continuous process of internal evaluation.

Detailed Lists and Practices

Some prayers and practices that can be recited while engaging in self-accountability are as follows:

  • Prayer 1: "Allahümme innî es’elüke afven ve âfiyeten fî dîni ve dünyâi ve ehli ve mâli."
    Recitation: O Allah! I seek Your forgiveness and well-being regarding my religion, my worldly affairs, my family, and my wealth.
    Translation: This prayer can be recited to seek Allah's help in every aspect of daily life.
  • Prayer 2: "Rabbena atina fid-dünya haseneten ve fil-âhireti haseneten ve kına azâbennâr."
    Recitation: Our Lord! Grant us goodness in this world and goodness in the Hereafter, and protect us from the torment of the Fire.
    Translation: This prayer can be repeated every night to establish a balance between this world and the Hereafter.

Lessons from Scholars

The great Islamic scholar Imam Ghazali frequently emphasized the importance of self-accountability. Ghazali would ask himself the following questions every night before sleeping:

  • How much did I struggle with my soul today?
  • What bad traits did I correct?
  • What good deeds did I perform in the way of Allah?

These questions held significant importance in Ghazali's spiritual development and serve as an example for contemporary Muslims.

Conclusion

At the end of each day, evaluating ourselves, recognizing our mistakes, and learning lessons is one of the most effective ways to purify our souls. This inner journey not only makes us better individuals but also brings us closer to our Lord. O Allah, grant us the will and strength to reckon with our souls every night.

baslik: The Patience of Prophet Job: A Lesson in Endurance Against Severe Illness and Calamities
seo_meta_aciklama: The patience of Prophet Job is a story filled with lessons that teaches humanity to be patient in the face of illness and calamities.
icerik_html:
Introduction

Each of us encounters difficulties in life from time to time; sometimes these challenges come in the form of health issues, and at other times through various calamities. However, what is required of us is how we stand in the face of these challenges. The Islamic faith advises patience and reliance on Allah. The story of Prophet Job provides us with profound lessons in this regard. His patience is not only a testament of a prophet but also a lesson that touches the lives of each one of us.

Historical/Religious Background

Prophet Job is mentioned as a symbol of patience both in the Qur'an and in the Sunnah. In the Qur'an, his illness and patience are expressed as follows:

"And remember Job, when he called to his Lord, 'Indeed, adversity has touched me, and you are the most merciful of the merciful.'" (Surah Al-Anbiya, 83)

This verse illustrates Prophet Job's devotion and submission to Allah. He struggled with a severe illness for many years and never lost his patience during this time.

Detailed Lists and Practices

By taking the patience of Prophet Job as an example, we too can seek refuge in Allah through supplication and worship. Here are some of the prayers that can be recited during this process:

  • Prayer: "Rabbi inne messeniye'd-durru ve ente erhamur-rahimin."
  • Pronunciation: "My Lord, indeed, harm has touched me, and You are the most merciful of the merciful."
  • Meaning: While reciting this prayer, we should reflect on Job's patience and open our own troubles to Allah.

Lessons from Scholars

Islamic scholars have consistently presented the story of Prophet Job as an example of patience and reliance on Allah. Imam Ghazali emphasizes the importance of patience and cites Prophet Job as a model. He states:

"The most beautiful example of patience is the state of Prophet Job. His exhibited patience teaches us how to stand firm against the difficulties of life."

Conclusion

The story of Prophet Job advises us to never lose our patience, regardless of what happens. We should resist the challenges of life with the steadfastness he demonstrated and increase our reliance on Allah. I hope that everyone reading this will take Prophet Job's patience as an example, seek refuge in our Lord's mercy, and remember Him in our prayers. May Allah grant us all patience and steadfastness.

baslik: The Chastity of Prophet Yusuf: A Faith-Filled Life from Prison to Egypt
seo_meta_aciklama: A comprehensive examination of the chaste, patient, and faith-filled life of Prophet Yusuf and his rise in Egypt, illuminated by the Quran and Sunnah.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The life of Prophet Yusuf represents one of the most beautiful examples of chastity, patience, and loyalty in human history. His journey from prison to the palace leaves deep impressions in the hearts of every believer. The experiences of Prophet Yusuf and his stance during this process offer us many lessons, both worldly and spiritual. In this article, we will discuss the faith-filled life of Prophet Yusuf from the perspective of the Quran and Sunnah.

Historical/Religious Background

The story of Prophet Yusuf is one of the most detailed narratives in the Quran. In Surah Yusuf, his prophetic journey, the jealousy of his brothers, his years of slavery in Egypt, and ultimately his rise to the position of vizier in Egypt are comprehensively covered. This narrative serves as an important commentary that reveals Allah's power and wisdom.

"Indeed, in the story of Yusuf and his brothers, there are lessons for those who seek." (Surah Yusuf, 12:7)

Detailed Lists and Applications

Some prayers and applications that can be derived from the life of Prophet Yusuf are as follows:

  • Prayer for Patience: "My Lord! Grant me patience and cause me to die among the righteous." (Surah Yusuf, 12:101)
  • Prayer for Chastity: "My Lord! The prison is more beloved to me than that to which they invite me." (Surah Yusuf, 12:33)

Recitations and Meanings of the Prayers

  • Recitation of the Prayer for Patience: "Rabbi hab li min ledunke sabren ve teveffeni muslimeen."
  • Recitation of the Prayer for Chastity: "Rabbi s-sijnu ahabbu ilayya mimma yed'uuneni ileyh."

These prayers can be sincerely recited after each prayer or in times of need.

Lessons from Scholars

Great scholars have drawn profound lessons from the story of Prophet Yusuf. Imam Ghazali describes Yusuf's patience and chastity as "the most beautiful adornment of the heart." Similarly, Rumi characterizes the story of Prophet Yusuf as 'the pinnacle of patience and reliance.' These scholars emphasize that in difficult situations, one should take Prophet Yusuf as an example.

Conclusion

The life of Prophet Yusuf serves as a guide and a beacon for every Muslim. His chaste demeanor, patience, and submission to Allah remind us that we should never lose our faith in Allah in any circumstance. We pray for everyone reading this article with the prayer of Prophet Yusuf: "My Lord! Include me among the righteous." Ameen.

baslik: The Companions of the Cave: Brave Youths Who Sought Refuge in a Cave to Protect Their Faith
seo_meta_aciklama: The story of the Companions of the Cave, a symbol of faith and resistance, is a great narrative mentioned in the Qur'an.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The story of the Companions of the Cave is a symbol of faith and resistance. These youths, who stood against the oppressive regimes of their time to protect their beliefs, are remembered as exemplary figures in the Islamic world. Their story is not only a historical event but also a spiritual journey. This narrative illustrates how we can preserve our faith in the face of difficulties.

Historical/Religious Background

The Companions of the Cave are mentioned in the Qur'an in Surah Al-Kahf. Their story speaks of a group of youths who sought refuge in a cave to remain steadfast in their beliefs. Allah protected them by causing them to sleep in this cave for many years and eventually awakened them. This narrative demonstrates Allah's power and His assistance to the believers.

"Or did you think that the Companions of the Cave and the Inscription were among Our signs a wonder?" (Surah Al-Kahf, 18:9)

Detailed Lists and Applications

The prayers of the Companions of the Cave are significant for those who wish to protect their faith. Here is one of these prayers:

  • Arabic: "رَبَّنَا آتِنَا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ رَحْمَةً وَهَيِّئْ لَنَا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا رَشَدًا"
  • Transliteration: "Rabbena âtinâ min ledünke rahmeten ve heyye' lenâ min emrinâ reşedâ"
  • Translation: "Our Lord! Grant us from Yourself mercy and guide us in our affair to what is right."
  • How to Recite: This prayer can be recited after the morning prayer or when seeking strength in the face of difficulties.

Lessons from Scholars

Great scholars have frequently mentioned the story of the Companions of the Cave. Imam Ghazali states that this narrative is one of the finest examples of patience and reliance on Allah. He emphasizes that the story should resonate in the hearts of every believer, saying, "The Companions of the Cave are a beautiful example of trust in Allah and patience."

Conclusion

The story of the Companions of the Cave reminds us of the importance of preserving our faith in the face of challenges and the strength of reliance on Allah. In our prayers, we should express their patience and trust in Allah. O Lord, grant us the strength and patience to protect our faith like the Companions of the Cave.

baslik: The Casting of Prophet Ibrahim into the Fire and the Greatest Example of Tawakkul
seo_meta_aciklama: Delve deeply into the story of Prophet Ibrahim's tawakkul and his casting into the fire.
icerik_html:
Introduction

Throughout human history, prophets have been sent to convey Allah's message to people, and their lives have served as guides for the believers. One of these prophets, Ibrahim (Abraham), exemplified one of the greatest instances of submission and tawakkul. His life, marked by devotion and faith in Allah, continues to leave profound impressions on our souls even in the modern age.

The casting of Prophet Ibrahim into the fire is one of the most striking examples of tawakkul in human history. This event teaches how believers should connect with Allah with complete trust. The submission demonstrated by Prophet Ibrahim continues to inspire contemporary Muslims.

Historical/Religious Background

In the Qur'an, the story of Prophet Ibrahim is narrated as a prophet who advocates for the existence and oneness of Allah. He opposed idol worship in a polytheistic society and was ostracized by his people for this reason. Prophet Ibrahim's courageous stance was a testament to his loyalty to Allah's religion.

"They said: 'If you are going to do something, then burn him and support your gods!' We said: 'O fire, be cool and safe for Ibrahim!'" (Surah Al-Anbiya, 68-69)

This verse describes how Allah protected Prophet Ibrahim and prevented the fire from harming him. Even before being cast into the fire, Prophet Ibrahim did not lose his trust in Allah and maintained his tawakkul.

Detailed Lessons and Applications

The prayer of Prophet Ibrahim serves as an important example of tawakkul and submission. Here are some lessons to be drawn from this story:

  • Tawakkul: Trusting in Allah and relying on Him in every situation.
  • Patience: Demonstrating resilience in the face of difficulties.
  • Prayer: Seeking help and guidance from Allah.

Prophet Ibrahim's prayer: "Hasbiyallahu lâ ilâhe illâ hû, aleyhi tevekkeltu ve hüve rabbü'l-arşil-azîm." (Meaning: "Allah is sufficient for me. There is no deity except Him. I have placed my trust in Him, and He is the Lord of the Mighty Throne.")

Lessons from Scholars

Many great scholars have taken Prophet Ibrahim's tawakkul as an example and advised following his path. Imam Al-Ghazali states:

"Tawakkul is the complete and perfect trust of the servant in Allah. The tawakkul of Prophet Ibrahim in being cast into the fire serves as a guide for the believers."

These words emphasize the significance of Prophet Ibrahim's tawakkul for Muslims.

Conclusion

The life of Prophet Ibrahim and the story of his casting into the fire serve as a unique lesson for believers. This event highlights the importance of trust and submission to Allah. We too should emulate Prophet Ibrahim's tawakkul in the face of challenges in our lives and strengthen our bond with Allah.

May Allah grant us a steadfast faith and tawakkul like that of Prophet Ibrahim. Ameen.

baslik: The Deluge of Prophet Noah: Unyielding Patience and Propagation Against the Denying Community
seo_meta_aciklama: The Deluge of Prophet Noah; a unique narrative of patience, propagation, and profound devotion to Allah. The struggle against a denying community.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The story of Prophet Noah is one of the most brilliant examples of patience, propagation, and faith in human history. This narrative is not only a past event but also a tableau of admonition that offers great lessons to contemporary Muslims. In the face of the challenges we encounter in our lives, the patience and determination of Prophet Noah provide us with spiritual guidance.

Historical/Religious Background

In the Holy Quran, the narrative of Prophet Noah is elaborately described in several chapters. In verses 25-49 of Surah Hud, the calling of his people to the path of Allah and the reactions he received in response to this invitation are mentioned. These verses emphasize his profound compassion for humanity and his unending patience. Furthermore, our Prophet (peace be upon him) has frequently highlighted the determination of Prophet Noah and his devotion to Allah in his hadiths.

"Noah called his people day and night; but his call only drove them further away." (Holy Quran, Surah Noah 5-6)

Detailed Lessons and Applications

The lessons and supplications derived from the story of Prophet Noah hold great significance for contemporary Muslims. Here are some applications that can be drawn from this narrative:

  • Supplication for Patience: "My Lord! Grant me the ability to be grateful for the blessings You have bestowed upon me and my parents, and enable me to perform righteous deeds that please You, and admit me by Your mercy among Your righteous servants." (Arabic: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَى وَالِدَيَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَالِحًا تَرْضَاهُ وَأَدْخِلْنِي بِرَحْمَتِكَ فِي عِبَادِكَ الصَّالِحِينَ)
  • Propagation: Be patient and compassionate when inviting people to the path of Allah. Remember that love and respect are the most important elements in propagation.

Lessons from Scholars

Islamic scholars have drawn many lessons from the story of Prophet Noah. Imam Ghazali has encouraged people to turn to Allah with patience and perseverance through this narrative. "Prophet Noah has set an example for humanity with his devotion to Allah and his patience. To be like him is our greatest duty," he stated.

Conclusion

The life of Prophet Noah reminds us once again of the importance of patience and propagation. By taking lessons from this narrative, we must remain patient in the face of the difficulties we encounter in our lives and never lose our faith in Allah. May Allah adorn us with the patience and determination of Prophet Noah. Ameen!

baslik: The Story of Prophet Musa and Khidr (a.s): Divine Destiny's Secrets Beyond Human Comprehension
seo_meta_aciklama: The story of Prophet Musa and Khidr illuminates the mysterious paths of destiny through a profound spiritual journey.
icerik_html:
Introduction

As human beings, we often ponder the secrets of destiny while traversing the complex paths of life. This inquiry leads us not only to our personal experiences but also to the stories that echo through the depths of history. The story of Prophet Musa and Khidr (a.s) is one of the most compelling narratives that allows us to explore the secrets of divine destiny that surpass human intellect. This tale serves as a guide not only for the journey between a prophet and a sage but also for anyone seeking the profound meanings of life.

Historical/Religious Background

In the Holy Quran, this story narrated in Surah Al-Kahf begins with Prophet Musa's quest for wisdom. Allah commands him to encounter a wise servant named Khidr and to follow the lessons he will learn from him. Prophet Musa encounters three significant events alongside Khidr: the damaging of a ship, the killing of a child, and the rebuilding of a wall.

"The verses 65-82 of Surah Al-Kahf detail this story and provide us with profound lessons on patience, knowledge, and the complexities of destiny."
