May Allah make us among those who give charity and adorn our lives with the beauties of charity. Ameen.

baslik: The Purification of Wealth through Zakat: The Economy of Islam Built on Sharing
seo_meta_aciklama: Zakat is the cornerstone of Islam's sharing economy, which purifies wealth and ensures social justice.
icerik_html:
Introduction

The religion of Islam offers a way of life that encompasses individuals both materially and spiritually. One of the most important elements of this way of life is zakat. Zakat is not only an act of worship but also a means of ensuring social solidarity and economic justice. It provides an opportunity for the purification of wealth and the cleansing of hearts. Giving zakat brings peace and happiness to the owner of the wealth while offering hope and support to those in need.

Historical/Religious Background

Zakat is one of the five fundamental pillars of Islam and has been emphasized numerous times in the Qur'an. Allah the Exalted has commanded the believers to give zakat by stating, "

And establish prayer and give zakat..." (Al-Baqarah, 2:43)
The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) also highlighted the aspect of this worship that strengthens social bonds by saying, "
Zakat is the bridge of Islam." (Bukhari)

Detailed Lists and Applications

  • Calculation of Zakat: The wealth must reach the nisab amount and a year must pass over it.
  • Who to Give It To: The poor, the needy, debtors, stranded travelers, and those striving in the way of Allah.
  • Zakat Supplication: "Allahümme tahhir malina bi-zekati ve barik lena fima a'taytina." (O Allah, purify our wealth with zakat and grant us blessings in the bounties You have given us.)

Lessons from Scholars

The great scholar Imam Ghazali stated that zakat frees a person from stinginess and purifies the heart. One day, when a student asked him, "Does giving zakat decrease my wealth?" Imam Ghazali replied, "Zakat is the insurance of wealth; it protects it both in this world and in the Hereafter."

Conclusion

Zakat is a great blessing that Allah has bestowed upon His servants. Through this act of worship, we purify both ourselves and our community. Let us act according to the advice of our beloved Prophet, "The hand that gives is above the hand that takes." (Muslim) May our Lord make us among those who purify our hearts along with our wealth. Ameen.

baslik: The Spiritual Destruction of Usury: What Does It Mean to Declare War on Allah and His Messenger?
seo_meta_aciklama: A comprehensive examination of the spiritual effects of usury in Islam and the profound meaning of declaring war on Allah.
icerik_html:
INTRODUCTION

While usury is a financial practice frequently encountered in the modern world, it is regarded in Islamic belief as a factor that leads to profound spiritual destruction. For many Muslims, usury is not merely an economic issue but also a part of a spiritual war. To understand what it means to declare war on Allah and His Messenger, it is necessary to delve into the spiritual depths of this subject.

HISTORICAL/RELIGIOUS BACKGROUND

In the Holy Quran, usury is expressed with the term 'ribâ' and is prohibited by many verses. Allah the Exalted states in Surah Al-Baqarah, verse 275:

"Those who consume usury cannot stand on the Day of Resurrection except as one stands who is being beaten by Satan into insanity. That is because they say, 'Trade is just like usury.' But Allah has permitted trade and has forbidden usury."

This verse strikingly reveals the spiritual and moral dimensions of usury. Usury is not only an economic gain but also a calamity that inflicts deep wounds on the human soul.

DETAILED LISTS AND APPLICATIONS

  • Supplication 1: "Allahümme inni eûzu bike mine'l-fâizi."
  • Pronunciation: "O Allah, I seek refuge in You from usury."
  • Meaning: It expresses seeking refuge in Allah from usury.
  • Application: This supplication can be recited after daily prayers to seek spiritual protection.

LESSONS FROM SCHOLARS

Imam Ghazali states that usury undermines social justice and diverts people from the moral path. His words indicate that usury is not only an individual calamity but also a societal destruction:

"Usury darkens the heart of man and destroys the integrity of society."

These words help us deeply understand the spiritual dimension and societal effects of usury.

CONCLUSION

In Islam, usury is not merely an economic issue but also a spiritual war. To protect our hearts from this destruction, we must seek refuge in Allah through prayer and worship. Our Lord, protect us from the darkness of usury and guide us on the right path. Every Muslim should fulfill their duty in this matter, walking towards the hereafter with a pure heart. O Allah, protect us from usury and its spiritual destruction.

baslik: The Destruction of Lying: The Most Dangerous Habit that Undermines Faith
seo_meta_aciklama: The destructive effects of lying on our spiritual world, the place of lying in Islam, and the insightful words of great scholars.
icerik_html:
Introduction

By nature, humans desire to lead a life of truth and honesty, yet they may sometimes deviate from this path. Lying is one of the most dangerous deviations. It profoundly affects not only social relationships but also our spiritual lives. The destruction caused by lying resonates deep within our souls and undermines our faith. In this article, we will thoroughly examine the destructive effects of lying on our spiritual world and explore ways to overcome this habit.

Historical/Religious Background

Lying is addressed seriously in the Qur'an and Sunnah. The Qur'an emphasizes the evil of lying repeatedly and highlights the importance of truthfulness and honesty. Allah the Exalted states, "O you who have believed, fear Allah and be with the truthful." (At-Tawbah, 119) indicating that truthfulness is an indispensable virtue for the believers.

"Lying is a sign of hypocrisy. There are three signs of a hypocrite: when he speaks, he lies; when he makes a promise, he breaks it; and when entrusted, he betrays." (Bukhari)

This hadith clearly demonstrates how lying can lead a person into a very bad situation. A Muslim should always embrace truthfulness and avoid lying.

Detailed Lists and Practices

There are certain prayers and practices to prevent lying. Here are some of them:

  • "O my Lord! Protect me from lying." This prayer can be recited after each prayer to safeguard one's tongue from falsehood. In Arabic: رَبِّ اجْعَلْنِي مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ
  • Every morning and evening, one can recite "La ilahe illallah". This brings the heart closer to Allah and helps one stay away from lies.
  • In our daily lives, we can strive to keep our tongues clean by constantly saying "Estağfirullah".

Lessons from Scholars

Islamic scholars have conveyed numerous insightful stories and sayings regarding the harms of lying. The great scholar Imam Ghazali stated, "Lying causes the heart to darken and distances a person from Allah." Imam Abu Hanifa, the founder of the Hanafi school, narrated the following story to illustrate the dangers of lying to one of his students:

"One day, a man thinks that lying is harmless and tells a lie. However, this lie leads to a chain of events and ultimately brings great calamity upon him."

This story shows that while lying may seem harmless in the short term, it can lead to significant problems in the long run.

Conclusion

Lying weakens not only our worldly relationships but also our spiritual bonds. To overcome this habit, we must seek refuge in Allah and always embrace truthfulness. May our Lord protect us from lies and all bad habits. Our prayer is that we may always be among those who speak the truth and are with the truthful. Ameen.

baslik: Consciousness of Death (Tafakkur al-Mawt): The Best Remedy to Avoid Being Ensnared by the World
seo_meta_aciklama: Discover the ways to avoid being ensnared by the world through the consciousness of death in the light of the Qur'an and Sunnah.
icerik_html:
Introduction

Death is an inevitable reality and the final destination of human life. Remembering this truth and living accordingly is the most effective way to avoid being deceived by the transient allure of worldly life. The consciousness of death both spiritually matures a person and elevates them morally. In this article, we will explore the spiritual significance of the consciousness of death and the profound meaning it brings to our lives.

Historical/Religious Background

In Islam, the consciousness of death is a frequently emphasized topic. In the Qur'an, Allah highlights the importance of death and the afterlife through various verses. For example:

"Every soul shall taste death. Then you will be returned to Us." (Ankabut, 29:57)

This verse reminds us that worldly life is temporary and that the true abode is the afterlife. Our Prophet (peace be upon him) also frequently advised reflecting on death:

"Remember often the destroyer of pleasures, death." (Tirmidhi, Zuhd, 4)

Detailed Lists and Practices

Some acts of worship and prayers we can perform to keep the consciousness of death alive are as follows:

  • Istighfar Prayer:
    Arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللّٰهَ
    Pronunciation: Estağfirullah
    Translation: I seek forgiveness from Allah.
    Practice: By reciting istighfar at least 100 times daily, we bring peace and tranquility to the heart.
  • Recitation of the Qur'an:
    It is very beneficial to read verses that describe death and the afterlife, reflecting upon them.
  • Charity and Good Deeds:
    Giving charity increases the consciousness of death and prepares us for the afterlife.

Lessons from Scholars

Islamic scholars and great figures have centered the reflection on death in their lives. Imam Ghazali stated, "Living with the awareness that death can come at any moment keeps a person constantly prepared and vigilant." Yunus Emre beautifully expressed the transience of worldly life by saying, "You will go to a handful of soil, why do you boast?"

Conclusion

The consciousness of death adds deep meaning and awareness to our lives. When we begin to live with this consciousness, we can chart a more spiritual and peaceful path without being ensnared by the transient pleasures of worldly life. May Allah grant us all a life filled with the consciousness of death and the readiness for the afterlife. Ameen.
